growth_records.medium: Artificial urine media (AUM) amended with meat extract, CaCl2, sodium oxalate

up.method: L. crispatus degraded significant amounts of oxalate (qualitative in vitro assay); no rate given

growth_records.cite: Agudelo et al. Cefazolin shifts the kidney microbiota to promote a lithogenic environment. Nature Communications (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41467-024-54432-6. [PMC11634958]
growth_records.snippet: The original AUM recipe was amended with 5 g/L of meat extract (OXOID) to facilitate growth of L. crispatus ... AUM was pumped to the chemostat at a rate of 1 ml/min and stirred at a rate of 150 rpm at 37 °C
growth_records.notes: Same CDC chemostat as E. coli record. D = 60 ml/h ÷ 350 ml = 0.171/h; µ set = D = 0.171/h nominal; td = 4.05 h. CAVEAT: biofilm reactor, µ=D is nominal (cells retained on coupons), no growth rate reported by authors. L. crispatus degrades oxalate (anti-lithogenic) - qualitative only, no flux value. Base AUM recipe in supplementary/ref 85. Low confidence.
